description: Implement keyboard input handling for grid-based player movement with turn resolution. The player presses a direction key, the character moves one tile, and then all game entities process their turn. This establishes the core turn-based feel.
acceptance_criteria: Given the player presses an arrow key or WASD, when the input is processed, then the player entity moves exactly one tile in that direction.
Movement into a wall tile or off the map edge is rejected with no turn consumed.
After the player moves, all other entities (enemies, systems) process one turn step before input is accepted again.
The player's facing direction updates to match the last movement direction.
A turn counter increments by 1 after each valid player action.
Input is debounced so holding a key doesn't cause rapid multi-tile movement — one keypress = one turn.
notes: Spec says 'Each action costs action points (AP). Enemies act simultaneously after the player commits their turn.' For MVP, start with 1 AP per move, expand later.
